89	\section{Coheight is preserved by open immersions}
90	\label{sec:coheight_topology}
91	
92	% Source pointer: \texttt{analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md} Decision 2 sketch
93	% L246--257 (the recipe extracts the lemma from a generic
94	% \texttt{Specializes.mem\_open} chain-lifting argument). The lemma is a
95	% generic topological fact about the specialisation preorder; no external
96	% reference shipped at \texttt{b80f227} states it under this exact
97	% formulation.
98	
99	\begin{lemma}
100	
101	\leanok
102	  [Coheight on opens equals coheight in the ambient space]
103	  \label{lem:coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding}
104	  \lean{Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding}
105	  Let \(X\) be a topological space carrying the specialisation preorder, and
106	  let \(U \subseteq X\) be an open subset. For any \(z \in U\),
107	  \[
108	    \operatorname{coheight}_{X}(z) \;=\; \operatorname{coheight}_{U}\bigl(\langle z, \, z \in U\rangle\bigr),
109	  \]
110	  where on the right \(U\) carries the subspace topology and $\langle z, \,
111	  z \in U\rangle\( denotes \)z\( viewed as a point of \)U$.
112	\end{lemma}
113	
114	\begin{proof}
115	
116	\leanok
117	  Both sides are by definition the supremum, over natural numbers \(n\), of the
118	  set of \(n\) for which there exists a strictly increasing chain
119	  \(z = a_0 \prec a_1 \prec \cdots \prec a_n\) in the relevant
120	  specialisation preorder (with \(\prec\) denoting strict specialisation). It
121	  therefore suffices to give an order-preserving bijection between the
122	  strict-generisation chains starting at \(z\) in \(X\) and those starting at the
123	  corresponding point of \(U\).
124	
125	  \emph{Chains in \(U\) embed into \(X\).} The inclusion \(U \hookrightarrow X\) is
126	  continuous, so it preserves specialisation: if \(y \rightsquigarrow x\) in \(U\)
127	  then \(y \rightsquigarrow x\) in \(X\), and distinct points of \(U\) remain
128	  distinct in \(X\). Hence every strict-generisation chain in \(U\) starting at
129	  \(z\) lifts to one in \(X\) of the same length.
130	
131	  \emph{Chains in \(X\) starting at \(z\) all lie in \(U\).} Conversely, suppose
132	  \(z = a_0 \prec a_1 \prec \cdots \prec a_n\) is a strict-generisation chain
133	  in \(X\) with \(a_0 = z \in U\). For each \(i \geq 0\), the relation $a_i \succeq
134	  z\( (in the specialisation preorder) is \)z \rightsquigarrow a_i$, i.e.\
135	  \(a_i\) is in the closure of \(\{z\}\) \emph{viewed from above}; in our
136	  convention the strict order \(a \prec b\) records \(b \rightsquigarrow a\)
137	  (the generic point is the strict maximum), so \(a_i \succeq z\) in \(X\) means
138	  \(a_i \rightsquigarrow z\) in \(X\). By the Mathlib lemma
139	  \texttt{Topology.Inseparable.Specializes.mem\_open} (any specialisation
140	  \(a \rightsquigarrow z\) with \(z\) in an open set \(U\) forces \(a \in U\), since
141	  \(U\) is open and contains \(z\), hence contains every point whose closure
142	  contains \(z\) --- equivalently every \(a\) with \(a \rightsquigarrow z\)),
143	  every \(a_i\) lies in \(U\). Thus the chain descends to a strict-generisation
144	  chain $\langle z\rangle = \langle a_0\rangle \prec \langle a_1\rangle \prec
145	  \cdots \prec \langle a_n\rangle\( in \)U$ of the same length.
146	
147	  These two operations are mutually inverse on the set of strict-generisation
148	  chains starting at \(z\) (resp.\ at \(\langle z, \, z \in U\rangle\)) and
149	  preserve length, so the suprema agree.
150	\end{proof}
151	
152	\section{Coheight on Spec equals height in PrimeSpectrum}
153	\label{sec:coheight_spec_duality}
154	
155	% Source pointer: \texttt{analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md} Decision 2 sketch
156	% L259--268. The convention reconciliation step traces to Mathlib's
157	% \texttt{spec\_le\_iff} in \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace} L438--447
158	% and to \texttt{Order.coheight\_orderIso} +
159	% \texttt{Order.height\_toDual} in \texttt{Mathlib.Order.KrullDimension}.
160	
161	\begin{lemma}
162	
163	\leanok
164	  [Coheight on \(\Spec R\) equals height in \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum} R\)]
165	  \label{lem:coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum}
166	  \lean{Order.coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum}
167	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring and let \(p \in \Spec R\) (viewed as a point of
168	  the scheme spectrum, with the specialisation preorder). Then
169	  \[
170	    \operatorname{coheight}_{\Spec R}(p) \;=\; \operatorname{height}_{\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum}(R)}(p),
171	  \]
172	  where the right-hand side is the supremum of the lengths of strict
173	  prime-ideal chains \(\mathfrak{p}_0 \subsetneq \cdots \subsetneq \mathfrak{p}\)
174	  ending at the prime \(\mathfrak{p}\) corresponding to \(p\).
175	\end{lemma}
176	
177	\begin{proof}
178	
179	\leanok
180	  The specialisation preorder on \(\Spec R\) is the dual of the natural
181	  inclusion preorder on \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum}(R)\). Concretely, for
182	  \(p, q \in \Spec R\) with underlying primes \(\mathfrak{p}, \mathfrak{q}\),
183	  the relation \(p \leq q\) in the specialisation preorder (which is
184	  \(q \rightsquigarrow p\) in topological notation) corresponds to the prime
185	  containment \(\mathfrak{q} \subseteq \mathfrak{p}\). This is the content of
186	  the Mathlib lemma \texttt{spec\_le\_iff} (located in
187	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace}, lines 438--447 at commit
188	  \texttt{b80f227}, with the explicit remark that the preorder on \(\Spec R\)
189	  equals the order-dual of the preorder on \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum} R\)).
190	
191	  Under this order anti-isomorphism, coheight of \(p\) in \(\Spec R\)
192	  (= supremum of strict-generisation chain lengths starting at \(p\),
193	  i.e.\ ascending chains \(p \prec a_1 \prec \cdots \prec a_n\) in the
194	  specialisation preorder) translates to height of \(\mathfrak{p}\) in
195	  \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum}(R)\) (= supremum of strict descending prime-ideal
196	  chains $\mathfrak{p} \supsetneq \mathfrak{q}_1 \supsetneq \cdots \supsetneq
197	  \mathfrak{q}_n$, equivalently strict ascending chains
198	  $\mathfrak{q}_n \subsetneq \cdots \subsetneq \mathfrak{q}_1 \subsetneq
199	  \mathfrak{p}$). The transport is mechanised by the Mathlib lemmas
200	  \texttt{Order.coheight\_orderIso} (coheight is invariant under order
201	  isomorphisms) and \texttt{Order.height\_toDual} (height in the order-dual
202	  equals coheight in the original order), applied to the order-dual
203	  identification \(\Spec R \simeq_o (\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum} R)^{\operatorname{op}}\)
204	  furnished by \texttt{spec\_le\_iff}.
205	\end{proof}
206	
207	\section{The coheight \(=\) Krull-dim-of-stalk bridge}
208	\label{sec:coheight_bridge}
209	
210	% Source pointer: \texttt{analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md} Decision 2 sketch
211	% L280--296 (the 5-step assembly recipe). Mathlib API surface consumed:
212	%   - \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.exists\_isAffineOpen\_mem\_and\_subset}
213	%     (\texttt{AffineScheme.lean:271})
214	%   - \texttt{IsAffineOpen.primeIdealOf}, \texttt{IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_stalk}
215	%     (\texttt{AffineScheme.lean:806--813})
216	%   - \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height}
217	%     (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Height:341--348})
218	%   - \texttt{Ideal.height\_eq\_primeHeight}
219	%     (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Height:45})
220	%   - \cref{lem:coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding}, \cref{lem:coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum}
221	
222	\begin{theorem}
223	
224	\leanok
225	  [Coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge for a scheme point]
226	  \label{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight}
227	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight}
228	  
229	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(z \in X\) be any point. Then
230	  \[
231	    \operatorname{ringKrullDim}(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}) \;=\; \operatorname{coheight}_{X}(z),
232	  \]
233	  i.e.\ the Krull dimension of the stalk at \(z\) equals the coheight of \(z\) in
234	  the underlying topological space of \(X\) (with respect to the specialisation
235	  preorder).
236	\end{theorem}
237	
238	\begin{proof}
239	  
240	  \leanok
241	  The proof proceeds in five steps, assembling Mathlib API rather than
242	  arguing from a single textbook reference.
243	
244	  \emph{Step 1: pick an affine open neighbourhood of \(z\).} By
245	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.exists\_isAffineOpen\_mem\_and\_subset}
246	  applied to the open set \(X\) itself, there exists an affine open
247	  \(U \subseteq X\) with \(z \in U\). Write \(\langle z, \, z \in U \rangle \in U\)
248	  for \(z\) viewed as a point of the subscheme \(U\).
249	
250	  \emph{Step 2: name the prime corresponding to \(z\) inside \(U\).} Since
251	  \(U \cong \Spec \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)\) via the affine equivalence
252	  \(U \simeq \Spec R\) (with \(R := \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)\)), the point
253	  \(\langle z, \, z \in U\rangle\) corresponds to a prime ideal $\mathfrak{p}
254	  \subseteq R$. In Mathlib this prime is named
255	  \texttt{IsAffineOpen.primeIdealOf}\,\(\langle z, \, z \in U\rangle\).
256	
257	  \emph{Step 3: the stalk is the localisation of \(\Gamma(U)\) at
258	  \(\mathfrak{p}\).} The classical affine-stalk identification says
259	  \(\mathcal{O}_{X,z} \cong R_{\mathfrak{p}}\). In Mathlib this is the
260	  instance \texttt{IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_stalk}, which witnesses
261	  \(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}\) as a localisation of \(R\) at \(\mathfrak{p}\) in the
262	  \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime} sense.
263	
264	  \emph{Step 4: Krull dim of the localisation equals the height of
265	  \(\mathfrak{p}\).} The Mathlib lemma
266	  \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height} gives
267	  \[
268	    \operatorname{ringKrullDim}(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}) \;=\;
269	    \operatorname{ringKrullDim}(R_{\mathfrak{p}}) \;=\;
270	    \operatorname{height}(\mathfrak{p}),
271	  \]
272	  where \(\operatorname{height}(\mathfrak{p})\) is the prime-ideal height in
273	  \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum}(R)\). By
274	  \texttt{Ideal.height\_eq\_primeHeight} this matches the
275	  \(\operatorname{height}_{\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum}(R)}\) used in
276	  \cref{lem:coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum}.
277	
278	  \emph{Step 5: lift back from \(U\) to \(X\).} By
279	  \cref{lem:coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum} applied to \(R\) and the
280	  affine identification \(U \simeq \Spec R\),
281	  \[
282	    \operatorname{height}_{\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum}(R)}(\mathfrak{p}) \;=\;
283	    \operatorname{coheight}_{\Spec R}(\langle\text{image of } z\rangle) \;=\;
284	    \operatorname{coheight}_{U}\bigl(\langle z, \, z \in U\rangle\bigr).
285	  \]
286	  Then \cref{lem:coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding} applied to the open
287	  \(U \subseteq X\) promotes the right-hand side back to
288	  \(\operatorname{coheight}_{X}(z)\). Concatenating the equalities from
289	  Steps~4--5 gives the conclusion.
290	\end{proof}
291	
292	\section{Codim-1 specialisation: synthesising \texorpdfstring{\(\operatorname{KrullDimLE} 1\)}{KrullDimLE 1}}
293	\label{sec:coheight_le_one_instance}
294	
295	% Source pointer: \texttt{analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md} Decision 2
296	% recipe scaffold L298--307 + Decision 2 hreg\_dim refactor sketch
297	% L319--340. This block is the consumer-facing wrapper of the bridge
298	% \cref{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight} for the codim-\(1\) case used in
299	% \cref{chap:Albanese_CodimOneExtension} and
300	% \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}.
301	
302	\begin{lemma}
303	
304	\leanok
305	  [Coheight \(= 1\) forces stalk \(\operatorname{Krull\,dim} \leq 1\)]
306	  \label{lem:ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one}
307	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one}
308	  
309	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(z \in X\) be a point with
310	  \(\operatorname{coheight}_{X}(z) = 1\). Then the stalk \(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}\)
311	  has Krull dimension at most \(1\), equivalently $\operatorname{Ring.KrullDimLE}\,1\,
312	  \mathcal{O}_{X,z}$ holds.
313	\end{lemma}
314	
315	\begin{proof}
316	  
317	  \leanok
318	  By \cref{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight},
319	  \(\operatorname{ringKrullDim}(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}) = \operatorname{coheight}_{X}(z) = 1\).
320	  The Mathlib predicate \(\operatorname{Ring.KrullDimLE}\,1\,R\) unfolds to
321	  \(\operatorname{ringKrullDim}(R) \leq 1\), so the inequality \(1 \leq 1\)
322	  discharges the goal.
323	\end{proof}

1	\chapter{The relative cotangent presheaf}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the relative cotangent \emph{presheaf} \(\Omega_{X/S}\) of a morphism of schemes \(f \colon X \to S\), starting from the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module \(\Omega_{B/A}\) already available in Mathlib. The output is a presheaf of \(\struct{X}\)-modules whose sections on each open \(V \subseteq X\) are the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map \((f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)\) induced by \(f\), where \(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\) denotes the \emph{presheaf-level} inverse-image functor (left Kan extension along \((\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}\)).
5	
6	The downstream role of this chapter is to provide the \emph{forward direction} of the standard smoothness criterion: smoothness of \(f\) implies that on every affine chart \(V \to U\) (with \(V \subseteq X\), \(U \subseteq S\) affine and \(f V \subseteq U\)) the appLE-algebra K\"ahler differential \(\Omega_{\Gamma(X, V)\,/\,\Gamma(S, U)}\) is a free \(\Gamma(X, V)\)-module of rank \(n\). This is the Jacobian-criterion direction and is what connects to the dimension hypothesis on the Jacobian: the smoothness of the curve \(C \to \Spec k\) in the Jacobian definition is the special case \(n = 1\), and the relative dimension of \(\Jac(C)\) is read off from the rank via the same forward implication. The bridge between this affine-chart-wise algebra-K\"ahler form and the section module of the relative cotangent presheaf \(\Omega_{X/S}\) was the former content of milestone~M1; the bridge declaration was excised iter-126 (zero in-tree consumers) and replaced in this chapter by the standalone K\"ahler-localization utilities of Section~REF, the off-loop Mathlib-PR work of which proceeds from \texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md}. The converse implication of the forward smoothness criterion is \emph{mathematically false} as stated and is documented separately in Section~REF.
7	
8	\section{The relative cotangent presheaf}
9	
10	\begin{definition}
11	
12	\leanok
13	[Relative cotangent presheaf of a morphism]
14	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
15	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
16	  Let \(f \colon X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes. The presheaf-level inverse-image presheaf of rings \(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S}\) on \(X\) (the left Kan extension of \(\struct{S}\) along \((\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}\), given on each open \(V \subseteq X\) by the colimit \(\mathrm{colim}_{W : \,f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)\)) is paired with \(\struct{X}\) by the canonical adjunction-transpose \(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S} \to \struct{X}\) of the comorphism \(f^{\sharp}\colon\struct{S}\to f_{*}\struct{X}\). Applying the presheaf-of-modules K\"ahler differential construction \(\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'\) to this map of presheaves of commutative rings produces a presheaf of \(\struct{X}\)-modules on \(X\), the \emph{relative cotangent presheaf} \(\Omega_{X/S}\).
17	\end{definition}
18	
19	\begin{lemma}
20	
21	\leanok
22	[Sections of \(\Omega_{X/S}\) on an open]
23	  \label{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
24	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler}
25	  
26	  For every open \(V \subseteq X\), the sections of the relative cotangent presheaf are, by construction, the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map \((f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)\) induced by \(f\):
27	  \[
28	    \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{\struct{X}(V) \,/\, (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)}.
29	  \]
30	  Here \((f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W : \,f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)\) is the presheaf-level inverse-image section ring, \emph{not} \(\struct{S}(U)\) for an arbitrary affine open \(U \supseteq f V\). The relationship between this colimit-source K\"ahler module and the classical ring-theoretic \(\Omega_{B/A}\) for \(A = \struct{S}(U)\), \(B = \struct{X}(V)\) on an affine chart \(V = \Spec B \subseteq f^{-1}U\) with \(U = \Spec A \subseteq S\) affine is the content of the bridge documented in Section~REF.
31	\end{lemma}
32	
33	\begin{proof}
34	  
35	  \leanok
36	  By definition of \(\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'\): the construction is built object-wise to be the Mathlib \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} module of the ring map at each open. The identification is therefore by \texttt{rfl} after unfolding the definition. The colimit description of \((f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)\) is the definition of \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} as the left Kan extension along \((\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}\) in Mathlib.
37	\end{proof}

39	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level \(\Omega_{B/A}\)]
40	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
41	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module \(\Omega_{B/A}\) together with the universal derivation \(\mathrm{d} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}\). The construction of \(\Omega_{X/S}\) on each open is, by definition, this Mathlib module applied at the ring level of the presheaf, so all ring-level Mathlib lemmas about \(\Omega_{B/A}\) transfer verbatim under Lemma~REF — but for the \emph{colimit-source} ring \((f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)\), not for \(\struct{S}(U)\). Transferring to the classical \(A = \struct{S}(U)\) source on an affine chart is the bridge of Section~REF.

44	\section{Smoothness criterion for \(\Omega_{X/S}\) (forward direction)}
45	
46	The forward direction of the textbook smoothness criterion identifies a necessary condition on the relative K\"ahler differentials: every point of \(X\) admits an affine chart \(V \to U\) over which the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{B/A}\) is free of the prescribed rank, with \(A = \struct{S}(U)\) and \(B = \struct{X}(V)\). This is the global version of the algebraic-side fact that an \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~\(n\)~algebra has a free K\"ahler differential module of rank \(n\). We state and prove the theorem directly on the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module, sidestepping the presheaf bridge of Section~REF. The converse implication (a local-freeness hypothesis on \(\Omega\) alone implying smoothness) is \emph{false}; see Section~REF.
47	
48	\begin{theorem}\leanok
49	[Smooth implies \(\Omega\) locally free (algebra-K\"ahler form)]
50	  \label{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
51	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega}
52	  
53	  Let \(f \colon X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(n \in \mathbb{N}\). If \(f\) is smooth of relative dimension \(n\), then for every point \(x \in X\) there exist an affine open \(U \subseteq S\), an affine open \(V \subseteq X\) with \(x \in V\) and \(V \subseteq f^{-1}(U)\), such that the K\"ahler differential module
54	  \[
55	    \Omega_{\Gamma(X, V) \,/\, \Gamma(S, U)}
56	  \]
57	  (over the appLE algebra structure \(\Gamma(S, U) \to \Gamma(X, V)\) induced by \(f\)) is a free \(\Gamma(X, V)\)-module of rank \(n\).
58	\end{theorem}
59	
60	\begin{proof}
61	  
62	  \leanok
63	  Fix \(x \in X\). We use the abbreviation \(A := \Gamma(S, U)\) and \(B := \Gamma(X, V)\) for the section rings on the affine chart we will produce; the algebra map \(A \to B\) is the appLE map \((f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}\) induced by \(f\).
64	
65	  \emph{Step 1: extract a standard-smooth chart at \(x\).} The auto-generated \texttt{mk\_iff} bridge
66	  \[
67	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} \quad [\text{verified}]
68	  \]
69	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth}; produced by the \texttt{@[mk\_iff]} attribute on the class \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}) unfolds the hypothesis \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~\(n\)~\(f\) into: for every \(x \in X\), there exist affine opens \(U \subseteq S\) and \(V \subseteq X\) with \(x \in V\) and \(V \leq f^{-1}U\), such that the induced algebra map \((f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}\colon A \to B\) satisfies \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~\(n\)~\(A\)~\(B\).
70	
71	  \emph{Step 2: pass to plain standard-smooth.} The lemma
72	  \[
73	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} \quad [\text{verified}]
74	  \]
75	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmooth}) yields \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~\(A\)~\(B\) from the relative-dimension-tagged form, unlocking the cotangent-module machinery in the next two steps.
76	
77	  \emph{Step 3: free K\"ahler module on the chart.} The Mathlib instance
78	  \[
79	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
80	  \]
81	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}) synthesises \texttt{Module.Free}~\(B\)~\(\Omega_{B/A}\) from \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~\(A\)~\(B\).
82	
83	  \emph{Step 4: pin the rank.} The theorem
84	  \[
85	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
86	  \]
87	  (same file as Step 3) gives \(\rank_B\,\Omega_{B/A} = n\) under the hypotheses \texttt{Nontrivial}~\(B\) and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~\(n\)~\(A\)~\(B\).
88	
89	  \emph{Step 4.5: discharge the \texttt{Nontrivial}~\(B\) side condition.} The chart witness \(x \in V\) shows that the open \(V \subseteq X\) is non-empty as a set, so its structure-sheaf section ring \(B = \Gamma(X, V) = \struct{X}(V)\) is non-zero by \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.component\_nontrivial} (the structure sheaf of a scheme has \texttt{Nontrivial} sections on a non-empty open). This supplies the \texttt{Nontrivial}~\(B\) hypothesis required by Step 4.
90	
91	  Combining Steps 1--4.5, the chart \((U, V, e)\) produced by Step 1 satisfies \(x \in V\), \(\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,U\), \(\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,V\), and under the appLE algebra structure \(A = \Gamma(S, U) \to B = \Gamma(X, V)\), the K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{B/A}\) is a free \(B\)-module of rank \(n\). As \(x \in X\) was arbitrary, the statement follows.
92	
93	  This is the forward direction of the Jacobian criterion: Stacks Tag~02G1 (and Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2 in the Noetherian case).
94	
95	  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} The five \texttt{[verified]} closure pieces the proof relies on are
96	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} (Step 1),
97	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} (Step 2),
98	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 3),
99	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 4),
100	  and \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.component\_nontrivial} (Step 4.5).
101	\end{proof}
102	
103	\begin{remark}[Lean naming]
104	  \label{rem:smooth_class_naming}
105	  The Lean class for ``smooth of relative dimension \(n\)'' is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension}; the spelling \texttt{IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension} that appeared in earlier iterations of this project is a deprecated alias in Mathlib \texttt{b80f227}. The \texttt{\textbackslash lean\{...\}} hint above names the non-deprecated form.
106	\end{remark}

109	\label{sec:bridge}
110	
111	The presheaf-form \(\Omega_{X/S}\) of Definition~REF and the algebra-K\"ahler form \(\Omega_{B / A}\) on an affine chart of Theorem~REF are connected, on an affine chart, by a canonical \(B\)-linear isomorphism. Earlier iterations of the project (iter-121 through iter-125) hosted this isomorphism in the Lean tree as a \emph{bridge} declaration \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_equiv\_kaehler\_appLE} backed by a Mathlib gap-fill sub-lemma \texttt{appLE\_isLocalization} (the load-bearing localization-at-the-image-submonoid claim, packaged as a multi-LOC residual sorry whose closure was concrete but had no in-tree consumer). The forward-direction smoothness criterion (Theorem~REF) closed iter-120 in algebra-K\"ahler form \emph{independently} of the bridge, so the bridge was project-local infrastructure with zero downstream consumer.
112	
113	\textbf{Excise decision (iter-126).} Per the iter-126 strategy-critic CHALLENGE, ratified by the iter-126 user hint endorsing "shortest path qualified by your role is to fill mathlib gaps", the bridge declaration and its load-bearing sub-lemma were excised from the Lean tree iter-126: closing the bridge produced neither a downstream consumer nor a Mathlib PR (the extractable PR candidate is M1.d — Lemma~REF below — and is \emph{independent} of the bridge). The two retained declarations in this section are the standalone K\"ahler-localization utilities that survive as upstream-PR-quality material; off-loop PR work proceeds from \texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md}.

115	\begin{lemma}\leanok
116	[K\"ahler module of a localization is trivial]
117	  \label{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
118	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
119	  Let \(A \to L\) be a localization at a multiplicative subset \(M \subseteq A\). Then \(\Omega_{L / A}\) is subsingleton (the zero \(L\)-module).
120	\end{lemma}
121	
122	\begin{proof}
123	
124	\leanok
125	  This is a thin re-export of Mathlib's existing infrastructure: \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of\_isLocalization}~\texttt{[verified]} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic}, line 303) produces the instance \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified}~\(A\)~\(L\) from any \texttt{IsLocalization}~\(M\)~\(L\) predicate; the instance \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton\_kaehlerDifferential}~\texttt{[verified]} (same file, lines 57--59) then yields \texttt{Subsingleton}~\((\mathrm{KaehlerDifferential}~A~L)\). Hence the project-side lemma is a two-line re-export (\(\mathtt{haveI} := \mathtt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of\_isLocalization}~M\) \texttt{; infer\_instance}); contra an earlier framing, no Mathlib gap-fill is required for this lemma.
126	\end{proof}
127	
128	\begin{lemma}\leanok
129	[Tower-cancellation equivalence for K\"ahler modules under localization]
130	  \label{lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}
131	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}
132	  
133	  Let \(A \to L\) be a localization (in the sense of \texttt{IsLocalization}), and let \(L \to B\) be a ring map forming a scalar tower \(A \to L \to B\). Then the canonical \(B\)-linear map \(\Omega_{B/A} \to \Omega_{B/L}\) induced by the tower is a \(B\)-linear equivalence.
134	\end{lemma}
135	
136	\begin{proof}
137	  
138	  \leanok
139	  The second fundamental exact sequence of K\"ahler differentials reads
140	  \[
141	    B \otimes_{L} \Omega_{L/A} \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{B/A} \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \Omega_{B/L} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
142	  \]
143	  By Lemma~REF, the left term vanishes (since \(\Omega_{L/A}\) is subsingleton and tensoring against it gives the zero module). Therefore the right surjection has zero kernel and is a \(B\)-linear equivalence. The Mathlib closure pieces are \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map}~\texttt{[verified]} (the exact sequence above) and \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.map\_surjective}~\texttt{[verified]} (the right-hand surjection), both in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic}; the project-side declaration packages these into a \texttt{LinearEquiv} via \texttt{LinearEquiv.ofBijective}. This is the most extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone~M1: a clean generalisation of \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale} to the ``only the lower step is formally unramified'' case, candidate name \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified}.
144	\end{proof}

151	\section{Converse direction (milestone M4)}
152	\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}
153	
154	This direction is recorded as an optional future milestone~M4 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}). Closure requires the deformation-theoretic hypothesis \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~\(A\)~\(B\)\texttt{)}, which the project may wire up if a downstream consumer demands the iff form. The remainder of this section documents the obstruction in concrete form: a counterexample showing that without that hypothesis the converse fails, and a comparison with the Mathlib converse lemma at the ring level.
155	
156	The reverse implication
157	\[
158	  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
159	  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
160	\]
161	is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that \(f\) is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of \(\Omega\) alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.
162	
163	\begin{remark}[Explicit counterexample]
164	  \label{rem:converse_counterexample}
165	  Let \(k\) be a field and consider the morphism \(f \colon \Spec k \to \Spec k[t]\) induced by the surjection \(k[t] \twoheadrightarrow k\) sending \(t \mapsto 0\). Then \(f\) is locally of finite presentation, its source \(\Spec k\) has a unique affine open (itself), and the K\"ahler differential module \(\Omega_{k/k[t]} = 0\) is free of rank \(0\) (the trivial \texttt{Module.Basis} indexed by the empty set witnesses this). Yet \(f\) is the closed immersion of a non-open point of \(\Spec k[t]\), hence is not flat and therefore not smooth, so \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~\(0\)~\(f\) fails. The local-freeness hypothesis on \(\Omega\) is therefore strictly weaker than smoothness, and the converse implication of the iff form fails on this very example.
166	\end{remark}
167	
168	\begin{remark}[The Mathlib converse lemma and its hypotheses]
169	  \label{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}
170	  Mathlib does provide a converse-direction lemma at the ring level,
171	  \[
172	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
173	  \]
174	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}). Its three hypotheses make the missing content explicit:
175	  \begin{itemize}
176	    \item \texttt{Algebra.FinitePresentation}~\(A\)~\(B\);
177	    \item \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~\(A\)~\(B\)\texttt{)} --- vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology, i.e.\ formal smoothness;
178	    \item a basis \(b \colon \texttt{Module.Basis}~I~B~\Omega_{B/A}\) whose range lies in the image of the universal derivation, \(\mathrm{Set.range}\,(\texttt{Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.D}~A~B)\).
179	  \end{itemize}
180	  None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``\(\Omega\) locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~REF would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~\(A\)~\(B\)\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is the content of milestone~M4.
181	\end{remark}
182	
183	\begin{remark}[Cross-reference to Stacks 02G1]
184	  \label{rem:stacks_02G1}
185	  The standard textbook biconditional ``\(f\) is smooth of relative dimension \(d\) iff \(f\) is flat, locally of finite presentation, and \(\Omega_{X/S}\) is locally free of rank \(d\)'' is Stacks Tag~02G1 (and is the Noetherian-case Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2). The flatness conjunct is precisely what is missing from a hypothesis that only asserts local freeness of \(\Omega\); supplying it amounts to the deformation-theoretic content discussed in Remark~REF. The forward implication proved as Theorem~REF above corresponds to the easy direction of the same tag.
186	\end{remark}

189	
190	Each bullet below is a future milestone whose Mathlib infrastructure is not yet available in the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227}; inclusion in this list does \emph{not} mean the project has abandoned it. The roadmap progression is: M1 (this iter) \(\to\) M2 / M3 (existence routes) \(\to\) M4 (converse, Section~REF) \(\to\) M5+ (sheafification, cotangent exact sequence, etc., listed here). Each item lists the deferred concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.
191	
192	\begin{itemize}
193	  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for \(\Omega_{X/S}\) (M5).} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf \(\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}\) on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis \(\Rightarrow\) sheaf on \(X\)'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is the deferred work for this milestone. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.
194	
195	  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence (M6).} For a composition of morphisms \(X \to Y \to S\), there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent \(\struct{X}\)-modules
196	  \[
197	    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
198	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
199	  \]
200	  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.
201	
202	  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section (M7).} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section \(s \colon S \to X\): if \(f\) is smooth of relative dimension \(n\) and \(s\) is a section, then \(s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}\) is a locally free \(\struct{S}\)-module of rank \(n\). The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled \(\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}\), which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.
203	
204	  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity (M8).} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C/k\),
205	  \[
206	    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),
207	  \]
208	  both sides being equal to the genus \(g(C)\). Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf, no trace morphism for proper morphisms, and no Zariski coherent cohomology of \(\struct{X}\)-modules, so the equality cannot yet be expressed within the project's Mathlib snapshot. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~III.7 and Stacks Tag~0FVU.
209	\end{itemize}
